zh.lifeSummary: 當David I King of Scots於1080年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾丁波時，他的父親Malcolm III King of Scotland49歲，母親St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland35歲。他於1114年與Matilda of Huntingdon結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1138年登記服兵役。他去世於1153年5月24日在英國英格蘭波藍德卡萊爾享年73歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾倫斯包霍利 特利尼迪 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Scots
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Possibly an altered form of Dutch Soodts, of uncertain origin.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When David I King of Scots was born in 1080, in Edinburgh, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Malcolm III King of Scotland, was 49 and his mother, St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, was 35. He married Matilda of Huntingdon in 1114. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1138. He died on 24 May 1153, in Carlisle, Cumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73, and was buried in Holy Trinity Church, Helensburgh, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Matilda of Huntingdon en 1114. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1138. Il est décédé le 24 mai 1153, à Carlisle, Cumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Holy Trinity Church, Helensburgh, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: David I King of Scots(이)가 1080년 영국 스코틀랜드 에딘버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Malcolm III King of Scotland(은)는 49세였고, 그의 어머니 St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1114년 Matilda of Huntingdon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1138년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1153년 5월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 쿠벌랜드 칼리슬일에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 던바토실르 헬스버그 홀이 트리니티 처르치에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Matilda of Huntingdon em 1114. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1138. ru.Church of the Holy Trinity, Dunfermline: холи тринити чурч, хеленсбург, дунбартоншир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда David I King of Scots родился в 1080 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Malcolm III King of Scotland было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda of Huntingdon в 1114 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. В 1138 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 24 мая 1153 г. в местности карлисл, кумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности холи тринити чурч, хеленсбург, дунбартоншир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando David I King of Scots nació en 1080, en Edinburgh, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Malcolm III King of Scotland, tenía 49 años y su mamá, St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Matilda of Huntingdon en 1114. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1138. Murió el 24 de mayo de 1153, en Carlisle, Cumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en Holy Trinity Church, Helensburgh, Dunbartonshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
